mission.title: confidential-containers: [RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Containers
mission.description: [RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Containers. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify confidential-containers feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your confidential-containers deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=confidential-containers
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=confidential-containers -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: [RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Containers

steps.title: Check current Pod configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant confidential-containers resources:
```bash
kubectl get pod -A
kubectl describe pod <name> -n <namespace>
```
Currently, we are facing some typical problems in CoCo.

1. How to configure KBS certificates every launch of TEE without embed certs inside guest images? This is not restricted to only KBS certs. Any

steps.title: Apply the fix for [RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Containers
steps.description:
This huge PR mainly aims to refactor Attestation-Service crate and binaries, including the following:
1. Changes the API of `verifier` and makes it as a separate crate
This would help other projects to import our different `verifier` plugins. Also this commit changes the API of `verifier` crate from
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
name: pod1
annotations:
  io.katacontainers.config.runtime.cc_init_data: 
```

steps.title: Confirm [RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Cont is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=confidential-containers -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: This huge PR mainly aims to refactor Attestation-Service crate and binaries, including the following:
1. Changes the API of `verifier` and makes it as a separate crate
This would help other projects to import our different `verifier` plugins. Also this commit changes the API of `verifier` crate from
```rust
async fn evaluate(
        &self,
        nonce: String,
        attestation: &Attestation,. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
name: pod1
annotations:
  io.katacontainers.config.runtime.cc_init_data: 

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with confidential-containers installed or the issue environment reproducible.
